page: Microsoft Azure D16ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv7 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the D16ls_v7 demonstrates strong single-threaded performance. It achieves top-tier status in single-threaded gzip compression at 35.31 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.95 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches an average tier of 106.76 GB/sec. For lightweight artificial intelligence workloads, the server processes text generation for a small 135M parameter LLM at a top-tier rate of 350.22 tokens/sec, while prompt processing for the same model averages 1893.25 tokens/sec. Other metrics, such as the PassMark CPU score of 30370.06 and Redis SET operations at 31.34 million operations per second, fall within the average performance tier.

The primary hardware tradeoff of the D16ls_v7 is its low memory density of 2.0 GB per vCPU and the complete absence of local storage, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. This resource allocation makes the instance highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not demand large memory footprints, avoiding the premium cost of unutilized RAM. It is suited for CPU-bound tasks, single-threaded file compression, static web serving, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ls_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv7 family, running on a Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs operating at 4.2 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, but supports premium storage. Performance benchmarks show top-tier results in single-threaded operations, including gzip compression at 35.31 GB/sec and small-model LLM text generation at 350.22 tokens/sec. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth fall into average tiers. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-intensive, low-memory tasks. It is best suited for single-threaded processing, static web serving, and lightweight inference workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ls_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs at 4.2 GHz, 32 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-threaded compression performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16ls_v7 is a compute-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and high-speed single-threaded processing capabilities.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and optimized memory allocation for CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs at 4.2 GHz
32.0 GB system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression
No bundled local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Optimized for static web serving, file compression, and lightweight LLM inference
